title: autobench.judge.disagreement v1
description: Escalation signal fired when the anonymous JudgingPool's dissent ratio for a single case exceeds the configured threshold (default 0.4). Indicates the case is contested — judges materially disagree on the verdict. Downstream consumers (AHE, promotion gating, calibration) should treat the matching autobench.judge.pool.verdict.v1 event with reduced confidence and may route the case to manual review or a higher-tier judge. Source: /autobench.

iteration.description: RSI iteration number (0 when not running in an RSI loop)

n_votes.description: Number of judges that successfully returned a vote for this case

consensus_verdict.description: Majority-vote verdict the pool would have emitted despite the dissent

dissent_ratio.description: Observed fraction of votes that did NOT match the consensus verdict. Strictly greater than dissent_threshold whenever this event fires.

dissent_threshold.description: Configured threshold the dissent ratio had to exceed for this escalation to fire (default 0.4 per nervous-bus-c48).

verdict_distribution.description: Map of verdict-string -> count of judges that voted for it. Sums to n_votes.

minority_verdicts.description: Distinct verdicts the minority of judges voted for (excludes the consensus verdict). Useful for routing — e.g. an OK consensus with a CE minority is qualitatively different from an OK consensus with a WA minority.
